userStories.title: Fix bridge bootstrap so NodeRuntime works outside vitest
userStories.description: As a developer, I need NodeRuntime.run() and NodeRuntime.exec() to work from standalone Node.js scripts, not only inside vitest.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify why the bridge IIFE (isolate-runtime.ts) fails to inject CJS globals (require, module, exports, process, console, Buffer) when NodeRuntime is instantiated from a standalone script via dist/ output

Fix the bootstrap so that `new NodeRuntime({ systemDriver: createNodeDriver(), runtimeDriverFactory: createNodeRuntimeDriverFactory() })` followed by `runtime.exec('console.log(1)')` works from a standalone `node --input-type=module -e '...'` script
Verify: `runtime.exec('console.log("hello")')` completes with code 0 and the onStdio hook receives 'hello'
Verify: `runtime.exec('const fs = require("node:fs"); console.log(typeof fs.readFileSync)')` completes with code 0 (require works)
Add a smoke test in packages/secure-exec/tests/ that imports from dist/ (not source) and runs a basic exec — this prevents future regressions
Verify kernel path: kernel.spawn('node', ['-e', 'console.log(1)'], { onStdout }) captures '1' — same bridge bootstrap, just through kernel dispatch
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: RELEASE BLOCKER. The bridge IIFE that injects CJS globals crashes during bootstrap when run outside vitest. Even `1+1` fails because the bootstrap itself references require. The vitest transform pipeline does something (likely TypeScript path resolution or module format handling) that makes it work. Check packages/core/src/generated/isolate-runtime.ts and how it's loaded by the runtime driver.

userStories.title: Fix module.exports capture in NodeRuntime.run()

runtime.run('module.exports = 42') returns result with exports === 42
runtime.run('export const answer = 42', '/entry.mjs') returns result with exports.answer === 42 (ESM mode)
runtime.run('module.exports = { a: 1, b: [2,3] }') preserves nested structures in exports
Verify from standalone script (not vitest): node --input-type=module -e 'import { NodeRuntime, createNodeDriver, createNodeRuntimeDriverFactory } from "./packages/secure-exec/dist/index.js"; ...'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: runtime.run() completes with exit code 0 but result.exports is always undefined. The export extraction in runtimeDriver.run() isn't capturing module.exports. May be related to US-008 bridge bootstrap issue — if require is undefined, module.exports assignment can't work either.

userStories.title: Fix hardcoded pnpm path for web-streams-polyfill in polyfills.ts
userStories.description: As a consumer installing @secure-exec/nodejs from npm/yarn, I need the web-streams-polyfill ponyfill path to resolve correctly instead of using a hardcoded pnpm monorepo-relative path.
acceptanceCriteria: WEB_STREAMS_PONYFILL_PATH uses createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve() instead of hardcoded relative path
web-streams-polyfill is listed as a dependency in packages/nodejs/package.json
polyfill bundling for stream/web and internal/webstreams/* works correctly after the change
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
## Reproduction
In packages/nodejs/src/polyfills.ts, WEB_STREAMS_PONYFILL_PATH was:
  fileURLToPath(new URL('../../../node_modules/.pnpm/node_modules/web-streams-polyfill/dist/ponyfill.js', import.meta.url))

This breaks outside the monorepo because:
1. The ../../../ depth assumes packages/nodejs/dist/ → monorepo root — npm consumers have node_modules/@secure-exec/nodejs/dist/, a completely different depth
2. The .pnpm/node_modules/ layout is pnpm-specific — npm uses flat node_modules/, yarn uses its own structure
3. web-streams-polyfill was never declared as a dependency — it was a phantom transitive dep that happened to be hoisted by pnpm

To verify: run `pnpm pack` in packages/nodejs/ and extract the tarball — dist/polyfills.js contains the hardcoded path but web-streams-polyfill is not in the dependency list.

## Fix
1. Import createRequire from 'node:module'
2. Replace hardcoded URL with: createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve('web-streams-polyfill/dist/ponyfill.js')
3. Add web-streams-polyfill to dependencies in package.json

acceptanceCriteria: packages/nodejs/package.json files array includes 'src/polyfills'

Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
## Reproduction
In packages/nodejs/src/polyfills.ts, resolveCustomPolyfillSource() resolves:
  fileURLToPath(new URL(`../src/polyfills/${fileName}`, import.meta.url))

After tsc compiles to dist/polyfills.js, this resolves to packages/nodejs/src/polyfills/*.js.
But package.json 'files' only listed ['dist', 'README.md'], so src/polyfills/ was excluded from npm publish.

To verify: run `pnpm pack` in packages/nodejs/ — the tarball does not contain any src/polyfills/ files.
All 13 custom polyfills (crypto.js, stream-web.js, util-types.js, internal-webstreams-*.js, etc.) are missing.
At runtime, bundlePolyfill() for any CUSTOM_POLYFILL_ENTRY_POINTS module throws ENOENT.

## Fix
Add 'src/polyfills' to the 'files' array in packages/nodejs/package.json.

US-099 fixed a hardcoded pnpm path in packages/nodejs/src/polyfills.ts. This story audits all other publishable packages for the same class of bug.

## Known acceptable patterns (not bugs):
- packages/nodejs/src/package-bundler.ts and packages/core/src/package-bundler.ts: these probe .pnpm/node_modules/ as runtime module resolution candidates — this is correct behavior for a module resolver that needs to find packages under any package manager
- packages/core/src/generated/polyfills.ts: contains pre-bundled polyfill source as string literals — .pnpm references inside are esbuild bundle comment artifacts, not runtime lookups
- packages/playground/secure-exec-worker.js: esbuild bundle with module ID comments containing .pnpm paths — not runtime lookups
- packages/secure-exec/tests/node-conformance/: vendored upstream Node.js test files with their own node_modules references

## What to look for:
- new URL('...node_modules...', import.meta.url) with hardcoded relative depth
- path.join/resolve with hardcoded '../../../node_modules' or similar
- Any runtime fileURLToPath() that assumes monorepo directory structure

userStories.notes:
## Context
US-100 fixed packages/nodejs missing src/polyfills/ from its files array. This story audits all 7 publishable packages for the same class of bug.

## Publishable packages to audit:
- @secure-exec/browser (packages/browser)
- @secure-exec/core (packages/core)
- @secure-exec/nodejs (packages/nodejs) — already fixed in US-100
- @secure-exec/python (packages/python)
- @secure-exec/v8 (packages/v8)
- @secure-exec/typescript (packages/typescript)
- @secure-exec/wasmvm (packages/wasmvm)

## How to check each package:
1. Build the package (pnpm --filter <pkg> build)
2. Grep dist/**/*.js for patterns like '../src/', '../lib/', '../assets/', import.meta.url with relative ../ outside dist/
3. Cross-reference against the 'files' array in package.json
4. Run `pnpm pack` and inspect the tarball contents

## Note:
Source map references to ../src/ are acceptable (they're debug metadata, not runtime paths).
Only flag paths that are used in fileURLToPath(), readFileSync(), require(), import(), or similar runtime resolution.
